items.title: World Cup fans BOO ‘ridiculous’ hydration break in Ghana vs Panama with rain hammering down and supporters in ponchos
items.summary: FANS at the World Cup match between Ghana and Panama appeared to BOO the hydration break - while the temperature was less than 20°C in Canada. It was announced before the tournament that two, three-minute intervals would take place during each game due to the extreme temperatures forecast. In some c

items.title: What are the patches on some shirts at the World Cup?
items.summary: England captain Harry Kane was seen wearing a special patch on his England shirt - why are they there and who else gets to wear them?

items.title: Kyle Walker praises Thomas Tuchel for tactic in World Cup win over Croatia that Gareth Southgate was reluctant to do
items.summary: WHEN I look back at the tournaments I played under Gareth Southgate, there is a difference compared to how Thomas Tuchel operates. items.title: Luis Diaz steals the show for Colombia in win over Uzbekistan
items.summary: In a World Cup where superstars have stepped up, Luis Diaz joined the party by registering a goal and an assist in Colombia’s 3-1 win over Uzbekistan.

items.title: From one to 48 - every World Cup team ranked after one game
items.summary: All 48 teams at the World Cup have now played once. BBC Sport's experts have ranked them all, from best to worst. Who is top? Not defending champions Argentina.

items.title: Ghana score late winner to break Panama hearts
items.summary: Ghana's Caleb Yirenkyi scored a 95th-minute winner to deny Panama their first-ever World Cup point in Toronto, Canada.

items.title: Colombia squeeze past dogged Uzbekistan to open World Cup campaign with victory
items.summary: Some very good things have come from Croydon, the often overlooked town in south London. The film director David Lean was born there, as was the singer Amy Winehouse, the actor Peggy Ashcroft and the sexologist Havelock Ellis. Roy Hodgson comes from Croydon. The Bill and Peep Show were filmed there,

items.title: From frustration to party time: Mexico ready for lift-off after steady start
items.summary: The opening night against South Africa was met with mixed feelings but Thursday night’s South Korea clash promises to spark feelgood factor The mood in Mexico City last Thursday night, after a 2-0 win over South Africa in the opening game of the World Cup, was gleeful. Fans crowded around the Angel

items.title: Amazing moment Jude Bellingham sings along to Wonderwall as England fans serenade team after winning World Cup start
items.summary: JUDE BELLINGHAM was filmed singing 'Wonderwall' as England fans serenaded players after the nation's opening World Cup victory against Croatia. The Three Lions cruised to a 4-2 win over Croatia on Wednesday following a second-half masterclass. Croatia came from behind twice following a brace from Ha

items.title: ‘If you can call it that’ – England sent World Cup warning over worrying state of MetLife pitch as top stars complain
items.summary: ENGLAND players have been warned about the worrying state of the MetLife Stadium pitch. The Three Lions conclude Group L against Panama next Saturday at the 80,000-seater venue just outside New York. And it will also host the World Cup final on July 19. But France ace Adrien Rabiot slammed the playi

items.title: Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi arrested for spot-fixing
items.summary: Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi, who is playing at the 2026 World Cup, was arrested for alleged spot-fixing just a couple of weeks before the tournament began, ABC News confirmed on Wednesday.

items.title: Portugal have a Ronaldo problem (again)
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo will go down as one of Portugal -- and soccer's -- best players, but in a disappointing World Cup opener, he struggled to have an impact for his national team.

items.title: Ronaldo frustrated as Congo stun Portugal in draw
items.summary: Portugal coach Roberto Martinez brushed aside concerns, about his team and about star Cristiano Ronaldo's goal-scoring drought, after Portugal's frustrating 1-1 draw with Congo DR.

items.title: Sources: Liverpool beat Newcastle to Muñoz deal
items.summary: Liverpool are set to sign Spain international Víctor Muñoz from Osasuna after hijacking his switch to Newcastle United, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Sources: Ex-Chelsea star Kerr to rejoin Gotham
items.summary: Gotham FC and Australian forward Sam Kerr have agreed to terms to bring Kerr back to the club and the NWSL, multiple sources told ESPN.

items.title: Kane leads England to statement win over Croatia
items.summary: England coach Thomas Tuchel said his players overcame their nerves and the psychological fear of losing a lead to power past Croatia at Dallas Stadium (AT&T Stadium) on Wednesday.

items.title: Pulisic training solo in race to be fit vs. Austra...
items.summary: United States national team attacker Christian Pulisic continued to train apart from the main group at Wednesday's training session, as he looks to recover from a calf injury he first sustained last week, and aggravated in last Friday's 4-1 over Paraguay.

items.title: Brazil WCup-winning coach Parreira hospitalized
items.summary: Carlos Alberto Parreira, who led Brazil to its fourth World Cup title in 1994, has been hospitalized in Rio de Janeiro for treatment of Hodgkin's lymphoma diagnosed in 2023.

items.title: Kane and Bellingham put on a show in England's thr...
items.summary: When have England been this fun at a World Cup? We react to it all: Kane, Bellingham, Tuchel. As for Croatia: Where was Modric?

items.title: Chicago to Chivas: Gutiérrez is the American leadi...
items.summary: Brian Gutiérrez was raised in Chicago and grew up with the Fire, but now has become a key cog in Mexico's World Cup campaign.
